229891.explanation: Local businesses are vital for employment, livability, and our economy. Just look around: at the football field or sports hall, you see sponsor boards from companies everywhere. Without these businesses, these clubs cannot exist.

229892.explanation: We at Gemeentebelangen have fought hard to grant shop owners the freedom to decide for themselves whether they want to open on Sundays. Thanks to Gemeentebelangen Dantumadiel, this is now possible!

229893.explanation: We find it important that the municipality supports good ideas from the villages. However, it is essential that there is support for them. Large wind turbines have no place in our landscape, in our opinion.

229894.explanation: We absolutely cannot justify that people who have just moved here get priority over our fellow villagers.

229895.explanation: We find it more important that good choices are made than that more money is spent.

229897.explanation: We believe that people who frequently need to use a toilet should be able to participate fully in society.

229898.explanation: We see this as the responsibility of the entrepreneur themselves.

229901.explanation: This is already happening, and we want to keep it that way. Music lessons for children are vital for the survival of local music associations.

229903.explanation: Municipalities in the Netherlands tend to take over police tasks. This is expensive and reduces investment opportunities for things like schools and roads. Moreover, police work is a specialized profession that requires an extensive selection and training process for good reasons.

229904.explanation: We find it important that the police know what is happening in the villages. For this, it is essential that extra police capacity becomes available.

229906.explanation: As a municipality, we must look at where the need lies. If the demand for social rental housing is low, it makes no sense to increase the number to 40%. Otherwise, we mainly build for people from outside.

229907.explanation: There is currently a large shortage of housing in the Netherlands. Although we must be careful with our landscape, we must also be realistic and accept that construction sometimes has to take place outside village boundaries.

229908.explanation: At present, 50% of the municipal budget is allocated to support people with low income or other vulnerable groups. Dantumadiel is already a very social municipality. We are eager to ensure that more people can find work.

229909.explanation: First and foremost, we need to look at how these people can return to work. If that is not possible, “socially meaningful work” is a good alternative.

229910.explanation: If we follow national policy, more and more people with disabilities end up staying at home. Work means well-being and provides structure to life.

229913.explanation: There is currently a major shortage of capacity in the power grid. This partly causes houses to be built later or not at all. More charging points means fewer homes. As far as Gemeentebelangen is concerned, homes should have priority over charging stations.

229916.explanation: Of course, this can be provided if availability exists, but it can never become a strict requirement. Besides the many other problems in the Netherlands, there is also a shortage of healthcare personnel. Unfortunately, it is not always possible to meet this demand. The most important thing is that people receive the necessary care on time.

229917.explanation: The price of treatment is not important; the successful result of getting a young person back on track is.

229918.explanation: Good accessibility of our villages is one of the key points of our party. This contributes to a safe living environment and promotes the development of village life.

229920.explanation: Recently, we have already addressed many kilometers of bike paths. We will continue doing so in the coming period.

229921.explanation: A proliferation of speed bumps and posts does not always make traffic safer, but it does make it less comfortable. So we want as few of them as possible.

229923.explanation: The focus should mainly be on what is actually needed. The annual cost is leading to us. If new construction proves to be cheaper and better, we choose that.

229924.explanation: We are proud of our Frisian language but also careful with our money.

229926.explanation: A lot of abuse is made of money intended for healthcare in the Netherlands. We want this money to actually be spent on care and not on an entrepreneur's Ferrari.

229927.explanation: Questions about care often concern very personal situations. You don't want fellow villagers looking over your shoulder in a village center.

229931.explanation: When spending public money, we always prioritize the general interest.

229933.explanation: We believe this belongs in national policy.

229937.explanation: Especially for temporary living arrangements, leasehold is a good solution. For building a large detached house, we think this is unwise.

229944.explanation: Large-scale refugee shelters do not suit our villages.

229945.explanation: There must be improvements in the management of our sports halls and canteens. How that happens is not important to us, as long as it is financially responsible.

229946.explanation: If this question arises, we believe the municipality should think along.

229891.explanation: Employment is important, but growth must fit the village and landscape. First fully utilize and further develop existing sites, with attention to accessibility and living environment.

229892.explanation: Peace, livability, and space for family life are important. Shopkeepers should keep their freedom but without an obligation for further expansion of Sunday opening hours.

229893.explanation: Generating energy together fits with stewardship and the strength of the community. The municipality can support initiatives with knowledge and financial incentives, provided projects are small-scale, affordable, and fitting with the village and landscape.

229894.explanation: Status holders have a right to housing, but the CDA also wants to do justice to home seekers from the local villages. It is necessary to build extra affordable homes so groups do not face off against each other.

229895.explanation: Good integration requires investing in language, work, and participation. This helps newcomers to join in and contribute to the community more quickly, and prevents larger social and financial problems in the long term.

229897.explanation: Good facilities are part of a welcoming and accessible municipality. More public toilets help the elderly, families, and visitors, and support vibrant village centers and recreation.

229898.explanation: Accessibility means participation for everyone. Through targeted support, the municipality helps entrepreneurs adapt their properties so that facilities remain accessible to all without placing an undue burden on entrepreneurs.

229901.explanation: Music lessons stimulate talent development and well-being. Through subsidies, every child can participate regardless of household income. This fits with equal opportunities and strong cooperation between education, culture, and associations.

229903.explanation: Safety is not solely the government's task. The CDA emphasizes responsibility and trust within the community. Cooperation with residents and police is paramount; extra enforcement officers are not automatically the solution.

229904.explanation: Low-threshold contact with the community police officer is important, but fixed weekly consultation hours in every village are not always efficient. The CDA opts for proximity and visibility tailored to the needs of each village.

229906.explanation: Affordable housing is crucial, but a fixed percentage can be restrictive. The CDA chooses customized solutions per village, with a good mix of social rental, affordable ownership, and housing turnover, matching local housing needs.

229907.explanation: The CDA wants to handle space and landscape carefully but recognizes that housing shortages sometimes require expansion. Infill where possible, expansion where necessary: always matching the scale, character, and support within the village.

229908.explanation: Poverty affects not only adults but also children who thereby receive fewer opportunities. By raising the income threshold, the municipality prevents exclusion, gives children from poor families more development opportunities, and allows every child to participate.

229909.explanation: Participation is important, but obligation should not be an end in itself. The CDA chooses activation with tailored solutions: encouraging where possible, supporting where necessary, with attention to health, caregivers, and prospects for regular work.

229910.explanation: Work must offer perspective. The CDA focuses on targeted, temporary subsidized jobs as a stepping stone, combined with guidance towards regular employment. Not more jobs for the system's sake, but customized solutions that allow people to participate sustainably.

229913.explanation: Electric driving requires sufficient charging options. By increasing the number of charging points, the municipality makes sustainability feasible and accessible without limiting residents’ daily mobility.

229916.explanation: Care is personal. Where possible, there should be room for choices that fit someone's identity and background. This increases trust and quality of care, as long as care remains accessible and affordable.

229917.explanation: Quality is paramount in youth care. It concerns appropriate, timely help for children and families. The CDA chooses professionalism and continuity; cheap solutions can be costly if problems worsen.

229918.explanation: The ring road improves traffic safety, livability, and flow in De Westereen. The CDA has supported this position for some time, with the precondition that the municipality does not bear the costs alone and that the province plays an active role in financing and implementation.

229920.explanation: Good bicycle paths improve traffic safety for students and recreational cyclists. The CDA wants to invest in wider, safer routes in rural areas so that cycling remains a safe and attractive alternative for young and old.

229921.explanation: Traffic-calming measures are sometimes necessary, but not always effective. The CDA opts for customized and smart solutions, such as shared spaces where appropriate, so that slow traffic has priority and public spaces remain clear.

229923.explanation: The town hall in Damwâld is a familiar and accessible place. Renovation is more sustainable and efficient than new construction and fits with careful use of public funds, maintaining proximity and good service for residents.

229924.explanation: The municipality adopts bilingual communication as a starting point. Residents and businesses also deserve a response in the language in which they approach the municipality. This is a form of respect, closeness, and reliability, fitting with Dantumadiel’s identity.

229926.explanation: Care funds are intended for people who truly need them. The CDA considers it important to prevent abuse, so resources are used efficiently and trust in the care system is maintained, with attention to humanity and customization.

229927.explanation: Low-threshold contact points help residents quickly find the right support. The municipality already works with contact officers for entrepreneurs and villages; the CDA also sees this as a logical and accessible step for care and welfare, with short lines and personal contact.

229931.explanation: Small facilities can be of great value to a village, but investments require careful consideration. The CDA chooses tailored solutions: the importance for livability and vulnerable groups weighs heavier than just the number of users.

229933.explanation: Home batteries can help with smarter energy use, but are still costly and under development. The CDA chooses first to provide good information and targeted pilots so that public funds are used efficiently and residents do not face unnecessary risks.

229937.explanation: The CDA is not fundamentally opposed to leasehold constructions, but these should not require a disproportionate amount of administrative capacity. Time and energy should mainly go to measures with more impact on affordable housing in Dantumadiel.

229944.explanation: Dantumadiel fulfills its legal responsibilities, but extra shelter beyond the legal obligation should not come at the expense of capacity, facilities, and cohesion in villages. The CDA chooses realistic choices that fit the municipality’s scale and possibilities.

229945.explanation: Clear and professional management has many advantages, but local involvement and volunteer power are valuable. The CDA is open to a municipal sports company where efficient, alongside room for customization and cooperation with existing organizations.

229946.explanation: Co-housing can offer opportunities but only when schools choose this themselves. The CDA believes schools must retain their own identity and that cooperation must be supported by the schools themselves, not imposed by the municipality.

229892.explanation: Sociaal Links believes the entrepreneur should decide this themselves. Unfair competition caused by a municipal border can never be the intention.

229893.explanation: Sociaal Links wants to support and encourage residents in developments concerning energy generation and savings. Initiatives that come from the grassroots have the greatest chance of success.

229894.explanation: No. Long waiting lists create a sense of injustice when groups are given priority.

229895.explanation: Sociaal Links believes newcomers should find work as soon as possible; Dokwurk can assist. Integration is about participation, not money.

229897.explanation: Sociaal Links fully supports this, especially because of calls from the stomach, liver, and bowel fund that for a large group this is a crucial facility for participation in society.

229898.explanation: Sociaal Links sees this as a responsibility of the entrepreneurs themselves. When it concerns public buildings or public spaces, however, the municipality must make every effort to ensure accessibility.

229901.explanation: Children must be able to discover and develop their talents. Sociaal Links believes music education belongs in the full curriculum.

229903.explanation: Sociaal Links believes that the enforcement officers who are in place should be deployed where they are most needed. Youth nuisance is a major problem that must be tackled firmly.

229904.explanation: Sociaal Links wants the community police officer to be visible; the officer knows the residents and the residents know him or her.

229906.explanation: Sociaal Links believes building should match demand. Many more affordable owner-occupied and rental homes and apartments must be built so that young people can rent or buy, elderly can move on, and homes become available.

229907.explanation: Villages should not grow into one another to preserve their identity. Every new construction plan requires careful consideration; it must fit the village, the environment, and its residents.

229908.explanation: Dantumadiel already does this, and Sociaal Links will ensure it continues. Sociaal Links believes people with work and education should be helped towards a better future. Especially children must be spared from the suffering caused by poverty.

229909.explanation: Work, education, or volunteer work provides meaning and/or a stable livelihood. Sociaal Links wants the municipality to collaborate with Dokwurk to offer people suitable training or employment.

229910.explanation: Sociaal Links believes that development company Dokwurk should help the long-term unemployed secure a job with a fair income so that this target group can build a stable life independent from the government.

229913.explanation: Sociaal Links thinks there is especially a shortage of charging points in the larger towns. More and more electric cars are appearing without the infrastructure being ready; in the coming years this needs to be addressed urgently.

229916.explanation: Care is funded with public money; to prevent fraud and exorbitant costs, Sociaal Links believes the municipality should always be able to oversee what is reasonable and fair.

229917.explanation: Sociaal Links wants to tackle care fraud firmly and decisively-no Ferraris for criminal care entrepreneurs in Dantumadiel. The quality of youth care must be affordable, appropriate, and high-quality with providers who have a heart for the target group.

229918.explanation: A market town like De Westereen must have appropriate access for residents and entrepreneurs. Traffic safety and traffic flows are concerning in several places in De Westereen and action must be taken in the short term.

229920.explanation: Completely agree!! Dantumadiel is wonderful to explore by bike but it must be safe. Sociaal Links believes investments should be brought forward and steps taken as soon as possible.

229921.explanation: Sociaal Links thinks roads within villages should be designed to discourage speeding. We prefer the roads and villages to look as nice as possible with as few speed bumps as possible.

229923.explanation: Public funds should be invested as much as possible in our villages and residents. A town hall must be accessible and functional with as limited an investment as possible.

229924.explanation: Language is identity and without identity you are nothing!

229926.explanation: Absolutely! Sociaal Links wants to tackle care fraud firmly and decisively-no Ferraris for criminal care entrepreneurs in Dantumadiel.

229927.explanation: Helping residents as quickly as possible prevents problems from worsening, limits personal suffering, and keeps it affordable for society. People come in more quickly when there is a nearby point of contact.

229931.explanation: Facilities are crucial for livability in the villages. Everyone should be able to exercise and enjoy recreation close to home; the wellbeing of residents is the priority instead of profit.

229933.explanation: The national government has encouraged households to install solar panels but now leaves them out in the cold with the end of net metering. Sociaal Links considers this unfair behavior; assistance with a home battery could provide support.

229937.explanation: By leasing, you can buy the land at a later moment. This gives people a chance to buy a home and take a first step into the housing market.

229944.explanation: Sociaal Links fulfills its legal duty appropriate to the nature and scale of our villages. Sociaal Links is against large-scale reception.

229945.explanation: Not a single company but customized solutions; every club is unique! We believe sports halls should be managed and maintained by the municipality or a company. Other sports facilities (e.g., football clubs) that want to manage themselves with municipal support should be possible.

229946.explanation: Sociaal Links believes schools and possibly more facilities belong together. We have already demonstrated this as a party in De Westereen, Damwâld, and Feanwâlden.

229891.explanation: Expansion is possible, but only appropriate to scale, landscape, and need.

229892.explanation: ChristianUnion stands for Sunday rest and wants to limit store openings. Rest for the family is central.

229894.explanation: The municipality takes responsibility for housing status holders.

229895.explanation: Good language skills, participation, and guidance are important for inclusion. This is funded by the national government.

229898.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate and reasonably access everything.

229903.explanation: Safety is important, but more enforcement officers alone is not a goal in itself.

229904.explanation: Low-threshold contact is good, but a weekly obligation is unrealistic.

229906.explanation: Many affordable homes are needed, but a strict norm limits tailor-made solutions.

229907.explanation: Infill development is preferred, but limited expansion is sometimes necessary.

229908.explanation: Existential security and tailor-made solutions are central.

229909.explanation: Participation is important, but stimulating is better than obliging.

229910.explanation: Participation jobs and Dokwurk help people participate.

229913.explanation: Fits with sustainable mobility and stewardship.

229917.explanation: Quality, proximity, and familiar faces come first.

229918.explanation: Traffic safety is important and should fit the landscape.

229920.explanation: Bicycle safety and sustainability require better routes.

229921.explanation: Reducing speed is good, and traffic calming where truly needed.

229923.explanation: A careful consideration is needed of how it will look.

229924.explanation: Frisian language is culture and identity.

229926.explanation: Abuse harms the vulnerable; supervision is necessary.

229927.explanation: A single counter and community workers are core policy.

229931.explanation: Small facilities can be valuable, but assessment remains necessary.

229933.explanation: So that the electricity grid is not overloaded.

229937.explanation: Can offer opportunities, but requires careful exploration.

229944.explanation: ChristianUnion takes responsibility and does not exclude extra shelter.

229945.explanation: ChristianUnion works through associations and a sports agreement; centralization is not a goal.

229946.explanation: Cooperation and clustering help maintain quality and proximity.

229891.explanation: The FNP wants to maintain existing business parks well and revitalize them where necessary. However, our own entrepreneurs must be able to develop and settle. Companies from outside can better locate in places with space and good infrastructure.

229892.explanation: The FNP wants to leave the choice up to the entrepreneurs themselves.

229893.explanation: We see the municipal government's role primarily in stimulating insulation in the existing housing stock. For (communal) energy generation, there are national schemes; the municipality does not need to contribute additionally. We do support creating spatial planning room, except for large wind turbines.

229894.explanation: Status holders must have a place. However, our own residents, especially our youth, also need housing. Therefore, the FNP believes more rental homes should be built. The municipality must make good agreements with the housing association "Thús Wonen" about this.

229895.explanation: The municipality must set frameworks for good shelter. This includes not only funding but also guidance and support by the community. In integration, more attention should be given to Frisian language and our culture, for example through a visit to the Sûkerei. The FNP supports guidance toward paid employment.

229897.explanation: The FNP supports more public toilets in Dantumadiel. This increases attention for people with bladder or bowel problems.

229898.explanation: That buildings must be accessible to people with disabilities is already legally established for new construction. The municipality has the duty to supervise this. The municipality must ensure that public buildings and outdoor public spaces are accessible for persons with disabilities.

229901.explanation: Initially, parents carry the financial responsibility for their child's music lessons. The FNP supports financial aid to low-income parents to enable their child's music lessons.

229903.explanation: Street safety requires more enforcement by the police. The FNP believes the municipality should not go overboard with regulations and supervision.

229904.explanation: A consultation hour with the community police officer can be a good way to identify and tackle problems early.

229906.explanation: This helps in the short term. For the longer term, the FNP wants to carefully assess the needs of home seekers. In terms of housing flow, the FNP also wants attention for suitable housing for the elderly.

229907.explanation: The FNP supports strengthening existing village centers before considering expansion on the village outskirts, but realizes this is not always possible everywhere.

229908.explanation: The FNP places greater importance on care and support remaining close by and not being provided by large organizations with much bureaucracy.

229909.explanation: The FNP wants everyone who wants and can work to have that opportunity. At the same time, it's good for people receiving social assistance benefits to contribute to society by doing socially meaningful work where possible.

229910.explanation: Those who want and can work should have the chance. Subsidized work must be meaningful, with prospects for regular employment.

229913.explanation: The FNP wants to focus more on sustainable transport. The municipality is responsible for charging infrastructure in public spaces. The FNP believes a few extra charging points are welcome.

229916.explanation: The FNP finds it more important that care recipients can be helped in Frisian, and that the care provider is as local as possible.

229917.explanation: Quality of youth care organizations is paramount. The municipality can steer this by requiring care providers to be local.

229918.explanation: Research must determine how De Westereen can be best reached and remain accessible. After that, the municipality can decide on a possible bypass.

229920.explanation: The FNP supports the construction of wider and safer bicycle paths. Construction can coincide with planned maintenance.

229921.explanation: Where traffic-calming measures contribute to traffic safety, the municipality should install them. The FNP opposes bollards and other dangerous traffic-calming measures on bicycle paths.

229923.explanation: The FNP opposes expensive new construction plans for the town hall. The town hall should remain at its current location and be modernized where necessary.

229924.explanation: Frisian is the mother tongue of our municipality. The municipality shows it takes its residents seriously by communicating in their language. The municipality can also consider this in recruitment and selection of new staff.

229926.explanation: The starting point is trust. We should not go overboard with more control and accountability. This does not mean that there should be no supervision. Abuse must be detected and addressed. However, we do not believe it should be stricter, as suggested in the statement.

229927.explanation: The FNP wants care to be close by for all residents of Dantumadiel.

229931.explanation: The FNP finds it important that smaller villages, such as Driezum, Wâlterswâld, Rinsumageast, and Broeksterwâld, also retain their facilities. There must be demand for these facilities.

229933.explanation: A home battery is interesting for people who generate solar energy. The FNP believes those people can decide themselves whether or not to invest in a battery. The municipality has no role here according to the FNP.

229937.explanation: This can be interesting to help first-time buyers get a home. The municipality owns land itself and should not hand over its position to project developers. The FNP wants the municipality to keep control.

229944.explanation: The FNP wants refugees to be housed mainly on a small scale, so they can be well accommodated within the community.

229945.explanation: Efforts should primarily support the management and volunteers of sports clubs.

229946.explanation: In the larger villages De Westereen and Damwâld this has already been realized. The FNP wants no obstacles in smaller villages for primary schools to be housed under one roof, as long as there remains a primary school in the village.

229891.explanation: Companies and entrepreneurs must have the opportunities to expand or start a business within their own municipality. This can be done by developing new business parks or by redeveloping existing business parks.

229892.explanation: The SGP holds Biblically principled reasons that shops should not be open on Sundays. There are also good social reasons for this.

229893.explanation: We believe that the municipality should encourage this.

229894.explanation: Not without limits. The municipality must fulfill its obligations. We want to be generous towards refugees with a residence permit. We prefer a mix of tenants.

229895.explanation: Not automatically. The first step lies with the institutions appointed for this purpose.

229897.explanation: As far as we are concerned, it is not really necessary.

229898.explanation: We believe that entrepreneurs can do that perfectly well themselves.

229901.explanation: This must be encouraged by the municipality. The costs for music lessons should not be a barrier.

229903.explanation: Safety must be guaranteed; if more community enforcement officers (BOAs) are needed for this, we will certainly support that.

229904.explanation: A consultation hour with the neighborhood police officer is important, but it does not have to be weekly.

229906.explanation: We find 40% social rental housing to be too much. More than the national percentage is not necessary in our view.

229907.explanation: First possibilities within the built-up area. Outside the built-up area cannot be done without more consideration.

229908.explanation: To combat poverty, the municipality must help them. This may also be arranged nationally.

229909.explanation: Where possible, people with social assistance benefits should play an important role in society.

229910.explanation: We believe the municipality should encourage this.

229913.explanation: We are in favor of expanding the number of public charging stations.

229916.explanation: We believe this freedom must be preserved.

229917.explanation: The municipality should pay attention not only to the price but also to the quality.

229918.explanation: A better connection of De Westereen and a connection to the Centrale As is good, but not at any cost.

229920.explanation: The current policy is sufficient. There is already enough investment in bike paths.

229921.explanation: Traffic calming measures must be useful and necessary. Otherwise, they should not be applied.

229923.explanation: We are in favor of renovating the existing town hall to make it future-proof.

229924.explanation: We believe the municipality does not always have to communicate in Frisian, but only where possible.

229926.explanation: More control is allowed to prevent misuse and fraud.

229927.explanation: Accessibility is good to reach residents. But having a point of contact in every village is not necessarily required.

229931.explanation: Facilities that are not used can be renovated or demolished in consultation with the residents.

229933.explanation: Since net metering is being abolished, it is good to help residents purchase a home battery. Partly to solve network congestion.

229937.explanation: This should definitely be one of the options.

229944.explanation: In principle, the municipality should not accommodate more refugees than legally required. However, if more accommodation is necessary, a solution should be sought.

229945.explanation: If the quality/price ratio is good, that is possible.

229946.explanation: Schools have the freedom to choose whether they want to be under one roof or not.

229891.explanation: BBB gives space to entrepreneurs but wants to handle scarce space carefully. New business parks should only be considered if it is logical and fits with livability and rural areas.

229892.explanation: Sunday opening was introduced recently. Reconsidering shopping Sundays is not on the agenda. We leave residents and entrepreneurs free in their choice on Sundays.

229893.explanation: BBB wants to stimulate local energy cooperatives and other initiatives for generating sustainable energy and greening homes, provided agricultural land and nature are spared.

229894.explanation: BBB wants that status holders do NOT receive priority in housing allocation and uses the Housing Act to prevent this.

229895.explanation: Good integration works best through meeting. By connecting newcomers with sports clubs, associations, and volunteers, real connections arise. That strengthens local clubs with new members, costs no extra municipal money, and builds on community and trust.

229897.explanation: BBB wants livable villages with sufficient facilities. Additional public toilets fit an accessible and welcoming Dantumadiel for residents and visitors.

229898.explanation: Accessibility is primarily the responsibility of entrepreneurs themselves, especially in new construction or renovation. This is a basic standard, not a reason for subsidies.

229901.explanation: Music lessons fit well with BBB, but organized smartly and not with expensive individual subsidies. A weekly music or singing lesson at school is efficient, affordable, and helps combat illiteracy because children read and sing together a lot.

229903.explanation: BBB trusts in the power of community and personal responsibility. First, we want to improve the visibility and accessibility of existing neighborhood police officers and BOAs, rather than immediately adding more positions.

229904.explanation: We want to further strengthen the accessibility of neighborhood officers and enforcement officers. Therefore, we advocate for fixed consultation hours, for example in village halls or community centers.

229906.explanation: BBB wants a good mix of owner-occupied, mid-range rental, and social rental housing. A fixed 40% quota can unnecessarily limit plans; it is better to determine per village and project what is needed for a livable, mixed neighborhood.

229907.explanation: BBB wants to use space sparingly. By primarily building within or directly adjacent to villages, we protect our characteristic scenic landscape and farmland and preserve the village character well.

229908.explanation: BBB wants residents to receive timely help, even if they earn just above the minimum income. A slightly higher income threshold and tailored solutions prevent problems from escalating into costly care and debts. This way, we help those who truly need it and keep costs manageable.

229909.explanation: BBB believes in responsibility and participation according to ability. Not everyone should be automatically required, but legitimacy must be monitored and expectations set where appropriate. Tailored approaches and trust take precedence over rigidity.

229910.explanation: BBB focuses on tailored solutions: sometimes a subsidized job may be appropriate, but massive expansion is not the answer. Preferably, invest in education and guidance so people find regular employment.

229913.explanation: Electric driving is growing, we see that. But at present, there is no demand; residents are satisfied with parking. Charging stations should be installed where demand arises. We will not routinely convert existing parking spaces without reason.

229916.explanation: BBB puts people first, not the system. Freedom of choice and tailored solutions belong to this, but within what is practical and affordable to organize in Dantumadiel.

229917.explanation: BBB opts for youth care that truly works. Good care providers with proven results prevent problems from worsening-which saves the municipality money in the long term and ensures a better future for our children.

229918.explanation: A ring road is expensive and harms the beautiful surroundings, while many residents do not share the problem. During the Foarstrjitte redesign, traffic safety must be ensured alongside maintaining traffic flow.

229920.explanation: Bicycle paths are essential for safety and accessibility in rural areas. BBB invests in good bicycle paths for cyclists of all ages so that cycling remains a safe and pleasant mode of transport.

229921.explanation: BBB recognizes that Dantumadiel is cluttered with speed bumps and obstacles on some routes, which complicates accessibility and livability. We advocate for fewer measures where they are not truly needed, but keep them where safety really matters.

229923.explanation: Two scenarios must be weighed: renovation versus new construction. Consider what is financially more attractive in the long term, including accessibility for visitors and employees. Make a well-considered choice.

229924.explanation: 90% of Dantumadiel speaks Frisian; therefore, bilingual communication is logical and appropriate. Although policy exists, it is not yet standard practice. BBB wants Frisian recognized as a full language in all municipal communications.

229926.explanation: BBB wants every euro spent on care to benefit residents who need support. Through careful monitoring, we prevent misuse and maintain room for warm, appropriate tailored care.

229927.explanation: BBB wants to organize help and support close to people, via village halls, area teams, and fixed walk-in moments. This makes care accessible and humane.

229931.explanation: BBB believes that facilities such as shops, sports facilities, medical care, village halls, and schools should be preserved where possible-because they are crucial for livable villages and social connection.

229933.explanation: Home batteries are storage, not energy savings. There is no national subsidy and the payback period is very long. BBB opts for affordable sustainability and opposes expensive plans with little effect.

229937.explanation: BBB wants to make affordable building possible and help young people stay living in their own village. Leasehold land can lower barriers for those who want to build but cannot immediately buy the land.

229944.explanation: BBB wants to reduce asylum inflow. The municipality should not accommodate more refugees than required. Housing must be proportional to the number of residents, respect the community, and avoid overburdening people.

229945.explanation: BBB values sports. When associations manage their own facilities, people stay involved and active. We support associations in all villages. This works better than a centralized sports company.

229946.explanation: BBB wants to prevent mergers and closures by supporting schools in collaboration. Think of shared subject teachers or shared facilities. This keeps schools accessible in every village.

statements.title: The municipality should allow fewer shopping Sundays. Currently it is every Sunday.
One municipal sports company should manage and maintain all sports facilities.
The current town hall should be renovated rather than replaced by new construction.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Dantumadiel should have more business parks.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must ask the police to hold a weekly meeting with the community police officer in each village.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de politie vragen om in elk dorp wekelijks een

titleParts.glossary: A fixed time when residents can ask questions or drop in.

statements.title: The municipality should support more people in poverty by raising the income threshold for assistance.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should invest more money in building wider and safer cycle paths in rural areas.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld steken in de aanleg van bredere en veiligere fietspaden in het

titleParts.glossary: Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
There must be many more subsidized jobs for the long-term unemployed in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Government-funded jobs for people who have little chance of getting a regular job.
People who have been without paid work for a long time (longer than a year).

statements.title: The municipality should routinely communicate with residents in Frisian as well as Dutch.
The municipality should invest in a bypass at De Westereen with a connection to the Centrale As.
At least 40% of the new homes built in Dantumadiel should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Dantumadiel moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should install fewer speed bumps, bollards and other traffic-calming measures.
The municipality should provide low-threshold contact points in every village for residents with questions about care and welfare.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should introduce a subsidy for home battery storage systems.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

statements.title: The municipality should not take in more refugees than legally required.
The municipality should make it possible to lease building land.
Dantumadiel should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should provide extra support to primary schools that want to be housed together under one roof.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should also invest in village facilities that are used by few people.
The municipality should more strictly check for misuse of Wmo and care funds.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet strenger controleren op misbruik van

titleParts.glossary: Social Support Act. The Wmo gives citizens the right to help and support so they can live independently at home as long as possible.

shootoutStatements.title: More public toilets must be installed in Dantumadiel.
